data.narrative:
Mach Industries is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Ethan Thornton that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $100M total raised as a proxy valuation, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis examines 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and one additional peer—with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B. The median across this set is $1.2B, reflecting market pricing for established frontier-defense players at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $695M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Mach Industries' competitive positioning within the peer cohort. Stage-progression yields a more conservative estimate, while peer-median anchors to market-traded valuations for comparable businesses. Resolving this range requires clarity on revenue scale, unit economics, and customer concentration relative to the identified peer set.
